---
title: "Where to Go from Here"
teaching: 30
exercises: 15
---

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: questions

- Where do I find help when I get stuck?
- What resources are available for continued learning?
- How do I access Dutch Caribbean data directly from R?

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: objectives

- Know where to find help: documentation, community forums, Stack Overflow
- Access Dutch Caribbean data using R packages (cbsodataR, WDI)
- Build a personal library of R scripts that replace SPSS workflows
- Understand the Carpentries community and further learning paths

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

## Getting help

TODO: Cover:
- `?function_name` and `help()`
- Stack Overflow `[r]` tag
- Posit Community (community.rstudio.com)
- R-bloggers
- Cheat sheets from Posit

## Dutch Caribbean data in R

TODO: Demonstrate:
- `cbsodataR` for CBS Netherlands StatLine data (BES islands)
- `WDI` package for World Bank data (all islands)
- Direct downloads from CBS Aruba and CBS Curacao

## Building your script library

TODO: Encourage participants to:
- Save every analysis as a script
- Comment what each section does
- Build a personal collection of "recipes" for common analyses

## Continued learning

TODO: Recommend:
- r4stats.com (Bob Muenchen) — SPSS-R comparison reference
- The Carpentries community
- Free online courses (Coursera, DataCamp)
- The DCDC Network as a peer support community

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: keypoints

- R has a large, active community — you are never stuck alone
- `cbsodataR` and `WDI` let you pull Dutch Caribbean data directly into R
- Save your analyses as scripts and build a personal reference library
- The DCDC Network is your regional peer community for continued learning

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
